$comment: THE DRIFT-GATED NODE-CATALOG (v1.6 — contracts-v1.md Contract 2 + Follow-up backlog). The seed source for the DRIVER-PROJECT SEED-CONTRACT hook (pi-runner/run.mjs `seedContract` op): for each downstream CHROME node-TYPE it declares { owns, bind, demand, tone, position } — `owns` = the node's section keys; `bind` = a DECLARATIVE derivation spec (DATA the generic op interprets, never code) of which frozen-blueprint sections/fields to gather into the resolved bind menu; `demand`/`tone` are templated against the same derived context; `position` is the parallel-stage ordinal. The hook resolves each template against the produced blueprint and writes blueprint.contracts.<node> = { owns, bind, demand, tone, ...extras }. ZERO game/archetype literals live here OR in the op — every concrete value (verb, ids, events, scoring/fail model) is drilled from THIS run's blueprint. Mirrors /tmp/seed_project.py (the hand-sim reference) byte-for-byte for shell+guidance; the node-TYPEs per the schema's $defs/nodeContracts are shell/guidance/asset/sound. Drift gate: each `owns` must equal the section keys the chrome producer writes (Shell→shell{}, Guidance→guidance{}, Asset→assetList-derived, Sound→sound{}); the op never invents a key absent here.

scalars.$comment: Extra top-level fields the op copies verbatim from the blueprint (additionalProperties:true on nodeContract permits them). Each: <outField> <= a dotted path with a default.

demand.$comment: Generic template over the derived context (see op). {scoring?A:B} = ternary on whether scoringModel != 'none'; {failResource} = ' + the <fm> resource' iff failModel not in {none,respawn} else ''; {gameOver?} = 'out' iff failModel in {none,respawn} else '' (renders 'with' / 'without' a gameOver branch).
demand.template: HUD surfacing the {scoring?score-progress:objective}{failResource}; title + how-to-play from controls[]; scene-flow with{gameOver?} a gameOver branch.

bind.$comment: guidance.bind = sorted(set(coreObservables)) + sorted(set(effects[].on events)) + sorted(set(custom[].emits[].name events)) + anchors(near:<id> then <id>.position over positioned entities) + ['milestone:<id>'...] + ['coreVerb:<verb>']. The teaching cue/trigger vocabulary; the second events segment lets guidance bind to custom-emitted events (the op resolves each `events` segment's single-string `from` independently).

scalars.$comment: guidance carries the positioned entity-id list verbatim (the diegetic cue handles).

demand.template: teach the core verb '{coreVerb}' safely at {firstMilestone}; announce the win-gate state-change; point a diegetic cue at the goal '{goalId}'.

bind.$comment: asset.bind = the slot list (each slot id, in assetList order ∪ entities[].assetSlot) — the slots the art lane realizes. Generic: pulled from THIS blueprint's assetList, never named here.

demand.template: real art for the {slotCount} assetList slots (one generated asset per slot, fit to its logical box).

sound.$comment: ACTIVE (v1.6) — the `sound` node-TYPE: `runSeedContract` seeds blueprint.contracts.sound, the Sound producer (sound-author) reads ONLY that contract + the bound event vocabulary, authors the sound{} section into spec/sound.fragment.json, and a per-lane DRIVER-MERGE fold folds it → blueprint.sound. ISOMORPHIC TO GUIDANCE: both bind the SAME event vocabulary (effects[].on ∪ custom[].emits[].name) — Sound is seeded by the Gameplay seed hook exactly like Guidance, NOT serial; one event name joins guidance (a coachmark) and sound (an sfx) as two INDEPENDENT consumers. It owns the blueprint.sound section. Drift gate: `owns` must equal the section key the Sound producer writes (sound{}).

bind.$comment: sound.bind = the named gameplay events (effects[].on) ∪ the custom-emitted events (custom[].emits[].name) — the SAME event vocabulary guidance binds. TWO `events` segments mirroring guidance.bind: the SFX layer keys each `on` off one of these names (the op resolves each `events` segment's single-string `from` independently). Generic: pulled from THIS blueprint's effects/custom, never named here.

demand.$comment: The thin prose ask (the bound event vocabulary lives in `bind`, not the demand — mirrors guidance, which references its handles via bind). No derived tokens: the renderer's plain-token grammar is {coreVerb}/{goalId}/{firstMilestone}/{slotCount}, none of which a sound demand needs.
demand.template: sound effects bound to the named gameplay events; one coherent SOUND PATH (the game's sonic identity).

observables.$comment: THE CORE OBSERVABLE PALETTE (Contract 4 — the live-__GAME__ guarantee). `base` = always exposed; `whenScalar` = additive observables gated on a drilled meta scalar's value. This is the ONLY place the failModel→observable mapping lives (DATA): a health game surfaces player.health, lives→lives, time→timeRemaining; respawn/none add no fail observable. scoringModel != 'none' adds maxScore. Used by BOTH shell+guidance `observables` segments so the core set is computed once per run from this blueprint's meta.
